Shape the low end with deep cello and contrabass lines moving patiently, supportive but never boomy. Feature string ensemble, solo viola-like phrases, muted piano, soft drones, and delicate harmonics; instrumental with no vocals, with fresh motifs and no existing soundtrack theme references. Use an atmospheric intro, motif-focused development, rising section, strongest climax, stripped breakdown, and spacious outro. Keep a controlled build toward release, expressive dynamics, and smooth automation so the cue feels cinematic without familiar cues. Mix with warm close strings, long hall tails, and restrained mastering, keeping low percussion, strings, brass, pads, and transient details separated.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, copied soundtrack themes, famous trailer cues, public-domain melody quotes, temp-track copying, clipping, muddy mix
